title: Boat Rental in Phuket: Costs, Seasons and Tips for 2026
html:
Renting a boat in Phuket typically costs from 8,000 THB for a half-day on a small motorboat up to 80,000 THB for a full-day crewed yacht. Chartered trips often depart from Royal Phuket Marina or Yacht Haven Marina. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Phuket or list your own.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Phuket?

Prices range from 1,500 THB per person for shared tours to 100,000 THB per day for luxury catamarans. Shared longtail boat trips to Phi Phi start around 1,500 THB per head. A private half-day on a 26 ft motorboat costs from 10,000 THB. Full-day charters on a 40 ft sailing yacht average 25,000, 45,000 THB. For a premium crewed motor yacht, expect 60,000, 100,000 THB. Compare verified listings for yacht charter in Phuket across all budgets.

Where to set off in Phuket

Most charter boats depart from Royal Phuket Marina, Yacht Haven Marina, Chalong Pier, or Bang Rong Pier. Royal Phuket Marina is the premier superyacht hub in the east. Yacht Haven Marina near Ao Po Grand Marina offers easy access to Phang Nga Bay. Chalong Pier on the south coast is ideal for day trips to Racha and Coral islands. Bang Rong Pier serves ferries and speedboats to Koh Phi Phi and Koh Yao Noi.

Which boat to rent

  1. Longtail boat, best for budget excursions to nearby islands, 8, 12 guests, from 3,000 THB per trip.
  2. Speedboat, best for fast transfers to far islands like Similan, 10, 16 guests, from 18,000 THB half-day.
  3. Sailing yacht, best for relaxed cruising around Phang Nga Bay, 6, 10 guests, from 30,000 THB full-day.
  4. Catamaran, best for spacious family charters, 12, 20 guests, from 45,000 THB full-day; see the Shangani Blue Lagoon 70 ft charter page for a 70 ft example.
  5. Motor yacht, best for luxury cruising, 8, 12 guests, from 60,000 THB full-day.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Phuket?

For bareboat charter of larger vessels you generally need an International Certificate of Competence (ICC) or equivalent. Most charter companies require proof of experience for yachts over 30 ft. For smaller boats or crewed charters, no licence is needed as a skipper is included. The operator confirms exact requirements at booking; this varies by vessel and company.

Visit Phang Nga Bay early in the morning to see James Bond Island (Khao Phing Kan) before the crowds arrive. The bay’s limestone karsts are most photogenic in low-angle light.

At a glance

  • Price band: 1,500, 100,000 THB
  • Peak months: November to April (northeast monsoon)
  • Trip length: 4 hours to 8 hours typical
  • Capacity: 6, 20 guests on private charters
  • Currency: Thai Baht

Questions about boat rental in Phuket

Can I book a same-day boat rental in Phuket?

Many operators accept same-day bookings if availability allows, but it is advisable to reserve at least 24 hours in advance.

What is the best season for a yacht charter in Phuket?

The high season runs from November to April with calm seas and clear skies, ideal for sailing to the Similan Islands.

Do I need to tip the crew on a chartered boat in Phuket?

Tipping is customary; 10% of the charter fee is a common guide for good service.

Are meals included in the rental price?

Most crewed charters include a lunch and soft drinks, though you should confirm with the operator before booking.

In 2026, renting a boat in Phuket remains one of the best ways to explore the Andaman Sea. Whether you choose a budget longtail or a luxury catamaran, Book via BoatHire24 to compare verified vessels. If you own a boat, you can list it to reach thousands of travellers: become a host on the platform.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Phuket and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
